history.content:
<thought>
The goal is not currently visible in my local view. The path ahead is blocked by lava, so I need to explore the surroundings to find a safe route. My current orientation is facing east, and the cell directly in front of me contains lava, making it unsafe to move forward. Turning left would allow me to explore the cells to the south.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
Given the current observation, the cell in front of me contains lava, making it unsafe to move forward. To the right, there are walls blocking any potential path. To the left, the cells are unseen. Since the goal is not immediately visible, I should explore the left side to see if there's a safe path.

I will turn left to check the cells to the west.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The current view shows that the cell in front of me is a wall, and turning left reveals more walls. To the right, the cells are also walls or unseen. Given these constraints, I should continue exploring to the left to find a potential path.

I will turn left again to check the cells further to the west.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The current view shows that the cells to the left and front are all walls or lava. There are no safe paths in the immediate vicinity. However, the cells above and below my current position are not explored yet, and they might contain a safe path to the goal.

I will move forward to explore the cell directly above me.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
Moving forward has revealed that the cell above me is a wall. Given the current layout, it seems that moving upwards is not leading towards an open path. I need to explore other directions to find a safe route.

I will turn right to check the cell to my right.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The current view shows that turning right did not reveal any new information since the cell to my right is still unknown (`?`). Given the previous observations, it seems the safest course of action is to continue exploring in the vertical direction downwards.

I will move forward to explore the cell below me.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
Since moving forward did not provide any new information and the cell directly below me remains unknown, I should continue to explore the vertical direction. Moving forward again will help me to uncover more of the map and potentially find a path that leads to the goal.

I will move forward to explore the cell below me.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
Continuing to move forward does not provide any new information as the cell below me remains unknown. It seems that the current vertical exploration is not revealing any paths or goals. I need to change direction to explore other areas of the map.

I will turn right to explore the horizontal direction to the right.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly to my right contains lava, so I cannot move there. The cell two steps to the right appears to be a floor cell (.), which seems like a safer option. Moving right will allow me to explore this potential path towards the goal.

I will move right.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
Moving forward did not reveal any new information; the cell ahead still contains walls. Given this, I should continue exploring horizontally to the right to find a viable path towards the goal.

I will turn right again to explore the next cell.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The previous move revealed a path to the right. The cells ahead now appear to be either lava or potentially safe floor cells. I need to proceed cautiously to avoid falling into the lava.

I will move forward to explore the next cell.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
